name: closed-loop-delivery
description: Closes the implementation-to-runtime loop so 'done' means evidence running in production, not diff merged. Five gates: implemented / reviewed / deployed / healthy-at-runtime / reachable-by-users. Pairs with verification-before-completion (PR-level) and pre-ship-gate (deploy-time); this skill owns the post-deploy 24h+ runtime evidence + user-reachability evidence that the other two don't cover.

positive_triggers.input: We're about to claim this slice is done — the diff is large. What closed-loop evidence do we need before declaring done?
positive_triggers.reason: Pre-done-claim + 'diff is large' = classic 'done = diff-size' rationalization → matches Use when 'about to merge a slice / feature / change and the natural impulse is diff is N+1, ship it'

positive_triggers.input: CI was green, pre-ship-gate Phase A+B passed, but the bug ticket just came back from a customer. Where did our loop close too early?
positive_triggers.reason: Bug surfaced despite green CI + pre-ship-gate → matches Use when 'after a deploy that had green CI / Phase-A+B pass but user reports the bug is still present'; the 5-gate framework has an explicit gap on Gate 4/5

positive_triggers.input: Establish a DoD for our team — 'done' should mean running safely in production, not just merged. Help me codify.
positive_triggers.reason: Definition-of-Done codification request → matches Use when 'establishing or auditing a team's Definition of Done'

negative_triggers.input: Review this PR diff for missing auth checks before merge
negative_triggers.reason: Per-PR review of code → matches NOT for 'PR-level evidence gate' → verification-before-completion routes here

negative_triggers.input: Within 5 min of deploy, audit the canary and CDN
negative_triggers.reason: Deploy-time audit (<5 min) → matches NOT for 'within-deploy-time evidence' → pre-ship-gate routes here

behavioral_evals.scenario: Engineer has just merged a slice that adds a 'bulk PII export' feature. Manager asks 'is it done?' Engineer reaches for closed-loop-delivery.
expected_behavior: §1 enumerates the 5 gates: implemented → reviewed → deployed → healthy-at-runtime → reachable-by-users
Gate 1 (Implemented) walked: PR + tests + slice review + verification-before-completion + security-and-hardening (PII handling = trust boundary)
Gate 2 (Reviewed) walked: review-work 5 lanes PASS; multi-slice requires each slice's verdict
Gate 3 (Deployed) walked: pre-ship-gate Phase A + B all green; specifically the feature-flag-state for the new export must be 100% (or planned rollout %), not stuck at 0%
Gate 4 (Healthy at runtime): sets the 24h+ monitoring window with specific metrics: error rate, p95 latency, alert count, saturation trend

Gate 5 (Reachable by users): feature flag state confirmed + cache invalidation confirmed + manual user-journey test confirms admin role can actually export PII (Gate 5 is the gap that explains the original ticket)
Output is closed-loop-evidence.md saved at workspace root with the 5-gate evidence chain
If any gate is FAIL, the task is explicitly NOT done; specific remediation for each failed gate is required

behavioral_evals.scenario: Engineer declares done within 5 min of merge. closed-loop-delivery refuses.
expected_behavior: Skill flags this as a strong indicator of the historical 'done = merged' anti-pattern
Gate 4 + Gate 5 cannot be PASS within 5 min of merge (24h+ monitoring window + user-reachability test)
Skill refuses to stamp 'done' until the 24h+ window has elapsed and Gate 5 has a manual user journey confirmation
Skill surfaces the risk: 'this is the same loop-closing-too-early pattern that produced the original customer ticket' (links to incident if applicable)

notes: Pairs with verification-before-completion (PR-level) + pre-ship-gate (deploy-time). This skill's specific contribution: 24h+ post-deploy runtime evidence (Gate 4) + user-reachability evidence (Gate 5). Source adapted from antigravity closed-loop-delivery skill. Per-PR cycle: ~24-72h depending on change scope. Quarterly cadence to audit whether DoD is being applied; not all PRs need full 5 gates (routine cleanups may merge without Gates 4/5).
